Hey guys, JB here, time for your daily motivation slash skills training. We're on customers for life here, right? It's the last module on certification, right? Just the beginning of your training. I'm just going to go on and on to you guys who are just, you know, getting this stuff like at the highest level. Also, remember, it's not just about becoming proficient technically with a straight line. That's pretty easy to get to. You know, three, six months, right? They're really getting absolute proficiency. But remember, it's also about showing up and using this at the highest level, you know, applying it. And that's an ongoing affair. You know, you never stop learning. You never stop growing. On some level, either you're growing or you're dying. There is no stagnation. You can't just stay the same, right? You grow or you die. So you're always going to be growing your knowledge base and so forth. That being said, let's get specific. I want to go through a specific strategy here. Really more of an overarching principle I want you to always remember. And that is that when you're out there either in the field, banging on doors, behind the desk, making phone calls, whether you're someone that's getting leads on the internet and you're just, you know, doing strategy calls and closing that way, you know, I don't know if you've ever been in a situation as a salesperson. I mean, you've had a really amazing referrer of business. I have always managed to find these triple A referrers. These are people who give you not just one, two or three, they actually almost make you the business of giving you massive referrals. And one client gives you 20, 30, 50 leads. And if you do 50, a new account, they start off as leads, but these people are basically pre-sold, right? Because the second 10 is already established, right? And by default, the third 10 and almost the first 10 is pretty well established as well. But that second 10, when someone refers to you, and then by default, your service that you're providing, it's triple A, right? You know, you're entering on the 95-yard line of a hundred-yard dash, basically, right? It's like almost cheating. Now, I'll tell you a story about a company. It was called Farron Tax in Long Island. Farron Tax. Farron Tax Services. F-A-R-I-N, real company, right? Nicest people in the world. I was out there doing door-to-door mortgages, as I've told you in the past, right? And I always had a couple of people I was training and taking them out into the field. I was making a ton of money, and I walked into this place and sure as my typical, get out, no soliciting required. Why would I ever want to do blah, blah, blah? And of course, 15 minutes later, they're my best friends. You know, using all the principles we've spoken about with the straight line system. But this one tax service ended up giving me no less than 150 referrals that ended up being clients with the average commission being about $8,000 to $10,000 per. Do the math. It's a million dollars. And it was every single year. This one company gave me that many referrals. Now, in certification training, right, there's a lesson that just strictly focuses on how you go out and get referrals. What's the system for? There's a strategy and a very concrete defined system that I use. But I want to tell you that that system is directly dependent on everything else you've learned before this. You can't just execute a referral system without knowing the rest of the straight line because so much of what we use to get referrals is essentially interconnected and anchored to the things that you did while you were closing, the questions that you asked, the active listening that you did that just made them understand that you truly cared, that rapport you built as a result of that, the way you created massive logical certainty and emotional something, where your loops were done in just the right way. It was the sum of all those amazing things that you did with the straight line that turned so on that maybe you said, yeah, fine, I'll do it. And they closed because you remember you're exceeding their action threshold. So someone would like to see a middle of not even me, but a middle of the road action threshold, 8.5 collective action threshold, right? So you got into an 8.6. They buy. That's a very simplistic measure here, but I'm just trying to give you some visual to think about here, right? So they're an 8.5 action threshold. You got into a collective level of certainty of 8.6. So they buy. Yes, for the order they say, yeah, let's do it, right? Is that person going to give you massive referrals on the spot? No way. They're not. In fact, when I go out there and I do my research, I always do it. But before I actually do a lesson, I always say is there anything else out there that I can add into this pickup? But if I find something that's great and there's someone who invented it, I'll give them credit. As you always know, I always say, I learned this from Banner. I learned this from this one, or that one, right? I always tell you, I say, Robert Sheldina. I always quote the people I learned from. I wish there was a lot more frequent than I actually learned something from someone when it comes to sales, but most of the stuff out there is utter garbage. But even worse, a lot of it is just toxically false. And that's what I'm drawing out with referrals. You see, if you go out there and look at what's the strategy of getting referrals and when's the best time to ask, you know what they say? After you've delivered the product and they've seen the benefits, that's the key time to ask referrals. Guess what? Now, what's a straight line? You know why? Because if you have someone with an 8.5 action threshold and you close them, you probably have a 9.89, 9.79, maybe even a 10, by the way. In other words, what we do through looping is we don't just look to slide through by the skin of our teeth and close that way. We barely exceed the action threshold, but most people without the straight line when they close, they've done that. They've barely created enough certainty to get the job done. You think that person's going to give you a referral? No way. They're barely questioning whether or not they made the right decisions themselves. The point is, why was I able to get all of these referrals always on the day I closed? It was never like I didn't call back three months later and get referrals. I didn't have to. And my people don't have to. You know why? Because we get them so certain and feeling so in tune with you, such tight rapport, logically emotionally, they could tell you care. They're more than happy to give you a referral. They're right there on the spot, the day you close them. Understand that? In other words, you don't have to wait some arbitrary time when you hear about that and you read about that from other places. You know what it's about? It's because they don't know how to close the right way using the straight line. So they eat out this close versus, bam, really closing with the straight line and getting a really, really, customers really sure about they're making a great decision. They're a future-paced emotion as well. That person gives you referrals on the spot. You get it? So I just want you to think about this. Because you're out there every time you close, I want you asking for referrals. Bottom line. Now, if you're not on certification, please just get on for your own sake at this point.
